response: Originating in the 1960s as a US Department of Defense research project, the internet evolved from the 1969 ARPANET demonstration through foundational milestones like the 1983 adoption of TCP/IP and Tim Berners-Lee’s 1989 invention of the World Wide Web. After becoming publicly accessible with the 1993 Mosaic browser and surviving the 2001 dot-com crash, it rapidly expanded through social media, cloud computing, and the 2007 smartphone revolution. Today, this continuous technological evolution has transformed a niche academic network into a ubiquitous global infrastructure that connects over 5 billion people and underpins virtually every aspect of modern life.
